title: Specifications of Constructed Diversion Dams in Iran (small-scale irrigation diversion structures, 1937-1967)
source_org: Iran Plan Organization (cited by World Bank appraisal mission)
source_url: https://openknowledge.worldbank.org (World Bank Archives, Iran country documents)
extraction_method: MANUAL TRANSCRIPTION with visual verification. Source: data/raw/world-bank-archives-iran/historical-documents/1975_water_supply_sewerage_vol2_annexes.pdf, Volume II Annex 4 Table 4.2, PDF page 32. Rendered to PNG at 300 DPI via `pdftoppm -png -r 300 -f 32 -l 32` and read directly with the Read tool -- this table (5 clean columns, no left/right split needed) was fully legible in the image with no ambiguous cells.
frequency: single cross-section (list of 15 dams with individual utilization dates)
units: length/height in metres, cultivation area in hectares
license_terms: World Bank Archives — public disclosure authorized document; underlying data originally compiled by Iran's Plan Organization
notes: Much smaller-scale structures than the major reservoir dams in the companion Table 4.1 dataset (wb1975water-major-dams-specifications-1971) -- these are diversion dams (weirs redirecting river flow into irrigation canals, not large storage reservoirs), spanning both the late-Reza-Shah era (Shavor 1937, Kheyrabad 1940) and the early-to-mid Mohammad Reza Shah era through 1967. Zabol/Zahak/Kohak entries reflect Sistan-basin irrigation infrastructure on the Hirmand/Helmand river system near the Afghan border. Part of this project's Round 38 (mining/dams/industrial subsectors) hunt.
